subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Calcination is a process in which ore is heated in the absence of air or air might be supplied in a limited quantity. 

Roasting involves the heating of ore lower than its melting point in the presence of air or oxygen.

2PbS+3O2→∆2PbO+2SO2

The above reaction is an example of roasting. Rest all the reactions are heated in the absence of oxygen.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: Both the assertion and reason are correct and the reason is the correct explanation for the assertion.
Both the assertion and reason are correct, but the reason is not the correct explanation for the assertion.
solution:
Iron can be extracted from Haematite ore. The Haematite is the mineral form of iron (III) oxide, one of several iron oxides.
Haematite is not a carbonate ore. Hence, the reason is false.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
The process of extracting Al metal by electrolysis of a mixture of purified Al2O3 and Na3AlF6 is known as Hall and Heroult process.
Graphite is used as anode which is useful in the reduction of alumina oxide to the Al metal. The graphite electrode reacts with the oxygen released at cathode to form carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide.
Therefore, the role of graphite as an anode in the extraction of Al is to check the formation of oxygen. In other words, we can say its role is to prevent the oxidation of Al back to Al2O3 by oxygen.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Both Statenent I and Statement II are false
solution:
Ellingham diagram was a curve that related the Gibbs energy value with the temperature. Ellingham diagram is used to check which metal oxide is reduced by which compound.

ΔG=ΔH−TΔS

The slope of the curve is -∆S

Since the slope of the curve is positive, thus the ∆S is negative. So the value of ∆S decreases left to right.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
solution: An Ellingham diagram is a graph showing the temperature dependence of the stability of compounds. Ellingham diagrams can be drawn for Sulphides, Oxides and Halides. The Ellingham diagram is a graph that shows how the ΔGf0 varies with temperature as a result of the formation of elemental oxides.

(b) Pig iron is formed in blast furnace

ores.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: decrease the temperature needed for roasting of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Cu</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math>
solution:
In actual practice, the ore is heated in reverberatory furnace after mixing with silica. In the furnace, iron oxide 'slags off' as iron silicate and copper is produced in the form copper matte. This contains Cu2S and FeS.

FeO+SiO2⟶FeSiO3

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
The following changes occur during smelting.

(a) Most of the ferrous sulphide gets oxidised to ferrous oxide which combines with silica (flux) to form fusible slag.

2FeS + 3O2 → 2FeO + 2SO2

FeO + SiO2 → FeSiO3 (Ferrous silicate)

The slag being lighter floats and forms the upper layer. It is removed through the slag hole from time to time.

(b) During roasting or in the blast furnace if any oxide of copper is formed, it combines with FeS and is changed back into its sulphide.

2Cu2S + 3O2 → 2Cu2O + 2SO2

Cu2O + FeS → Cu2S + FeO

Ferrous oxide thus formed again combines with silica to form more slag.

FeO+SiO2→FeSiO3

So, no formation of calcium silicate CaSiO3 in extraction of Cu.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Mond process for metal refining: In this process, nickel is heated in a stream of carbon monoxide forming a volatile complex, nickel tetracarbonyl:

Ni + 4CO →330 - 350K Ni(CO)4

The carbonyl is subjected to a higher temperature so that it is decomposed giving the pure metal:

Ni(CO)4 →450 - 470K Ni + 4CO

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is false but R is true
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
solution:
For line II ∆S is more negative than line I. In the Ellingham diagram, a sharp change in slope of the line is observed for Mg-MgO at ~1120oC because that is the boiling point of magnesium.

From diagram, ∆SI=(Ssolid)-(Ssolid+Sgas)∆SII=(Sliquid)-(Ssolid+Sgas)

There is a large increase in entropy associated with the change of state of magnesium.

So, both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).

solution: Markovnikov's rule of addition : In an electrophilic addition reaction to alkenes and alkynes the electrophile attacks in such a way that a more stable carbocation intermediate is formed on which nucleophile attacks in the next step.

At <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mo><</mo><mn>1200</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math>, the reaction <math><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>)</mo></math> is spontaneous

solution:
ΔG=Δ G 0 +RTlnQ

At Equilibrium ΔG=0  & Q=K

∆Go=-RTln⁡Kp

∴-ln⁡Kp=∆GoRT

∆Go∝ Temperature. At high temperature ∆Go value is very high so non-feasible or non-spontaneous process takes place.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
The Hall–Héroult process is the major industrial process for smelting aluminium. It involves dissolving aluminium oxide (alumina) (obtained most often from bauxite, aluminium's chief ore, through the Bayer process) in molten cryolite, and electrolyzing the molten salt bath, typically in a purpose-built cell.

In this process carbon electrodes are used.

The following reactions take place 

2Al2O3+3C→4Al+3CO2

solution:
Ellingham diagram is graph of ΔG° vs T of any metal/element oxide. since

ΔG°=ΔH°-TΔS°

for most metal oxide formation metal (s) + oxygen (g) →metal oxide (s)

ΔH°=-ve

ΔS°=-ve

so graph will be a straight line with - ve y-intracept & + ve slope.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Liquation method is used when the melting point of metal is less compare to the melting point of the associated impurity.

In liquation process, the impure metal is heated on slopy hearth of furnace, due to less melting point, metal melts first and flow down leaving impurities behind.

solution:
Liquation is used to purify the metal whose melting point is lower than that of impurities like Sn, Bi,Pb etc.
Mond’s process The impure nickle is heated in presence of carbon monoxide. 

Nis + COg →NiCO4g → Nis + 4COg
Zone refining is based on the principle that impurities are more soluble in melt that in the pure solid metal. The metals like Ga, Ge etc. are purified by this method.
Van Arkell method  is used for those metals which are very difficult to obtain in pure state like Zr.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Ellingham diagram is a graphical representation of ΔG vs T when metal heated with oxygen to form metal oxide.

An Ellingham diagram is a graph showing the temperature dependence of the stability for compounds. This analysis is usually used to evaluate the ease of reduction of metal oxides and sulphides.

Ellingham diagram represents change in free energy ΔG with temperature

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: Pig iron is obtained from cast iron.
The Hall-Heroult process is used for the production of aluminium and iron.

leaching of bauxite using concentrated <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></math> solution gives sodium aluminate and sodium silicate.

the blistered appearance of copper during the metallurgical process is due to the evolution of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>.</mo></math>
solution:
(1) During leaching when bauxite is treated with concentrated NaOH, then sodium aluminate and sodium silicate is formed in the soluble form, whereas Fe2O3 is precipitated
(2) The blistered appearance of copper during the metallurgical process is due to evolution of SO2.
(3) Cast iron is obtained from pig iron.
(4) Hall-Heroult process is used for production of only aluminium.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Mond's process involves the combination of carbon monoxide with nickel readily and reversibly and gives nickel tetracarbonyl. This process is used to extract and purify nickel.

The Van Arkel technique is used to refine Zirconium or Titanium. Impurities found in Zirconium or Titanium in the form of nitrogen and oxygen are eliminated using this technique.

Copper is purified by electrolysis. Electricity is passed through solutions containing copper compounds, such as copper sulphate. The anode (positive electrode ) is made from impure copper and the cathode (negative electrode) is made from pure copper.

Mercury and zinc are purified by distillation method. Liquation is a metallurgical method to remove lead containing silver from copper and remove antimony minerals from ore.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: (Aromatic)

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Electrolysis is not a concentration method. Electrolytic refining is a process of refining a metal (mainly copper) by the process of electrolysis.

Liquation is not a concentration method. It is a refining method. In this method a low melting metal like tin can be made to flow on a sloping surface. In this way it is separated from higher melting impurities.

The other three methods are concentration methods of ores.

Leaching is used when the ore is soluble in a solvent. The powdered ore is dissolved in a chemical, usually a strong solution of NaOH. The chemical solution dissolves the metal in the ore, and it can be extracted and separated from the gangue by extracting the chemical solution.

Froth flotation is a process for selectively separating hydrophobic materials from hydrophilic.

Hydraulic washing is a technique used when the impurities are lighter and the ore particles are heavier. The lighter impurities are removed by washing in current of water. As gold particles are heavier than the impurities like sand, we can use hydraulic washing for the concentration of ores of gold.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Calamine, Siderite and Malachite are carbonate containing ores. Sphalerite is a sulphide ore.

Calamine: ZnCO3

Siderite: FeCO3

Sphalerite : ZnS

Malachite : CuCO3·CuOH2
